name: create
context: Create new Linear issues with title, description, priority, labels, and team assignment
info: Creates issues in Linear via the `issueCreate` GraphQL mutation or the `client.createIssue()` SDK method. A title and teamId are required. Optional fields include description (markdown), priority (0-4), assigneeId, labelIds, projectId, cycleId, stateId, estimate, dueDate, and parentId for sub-issues. The mutation returns a success boolean and the created issue object with its identifier (e.g., ENG-42) and URL. Agents use this to file bugs from CI failures, create tasks from natural language, or automate issue intake from external sources. See [[linear/teams]] to find the target teamId.

sections.title: GraphQL mutation
sections.body: Use `issueCreate(input: IssueCreateInput!)` mutation. The input requires `teamId` and `title`. Returns `{ success, issue { id, identifier, url } }`. All UUIDs must reference existing entities. Invalid IDs return GraphQL errors.

sections.title: SDK method
sections.body: Call `client.createIssue({ teamId, title, ... })`. Returns a payload with `success` boolean and `issue` accessor. The issue accessor is a promise — use `await result.issue` to get the full issue object with `identifier`, `url`, `id`.

sections.title: Sub-issues
sections.body: Create sub-issues by setting `parentId` to an existing issue's UUID. Sub-issues inherit the parent's team but can have different assignees, priorities, and states. They appear nested under the parent in Linear's UI.

links.context: Find the teamId required for issue creation
Associate issues with projects via projectId

related.note: Must know teamId before creating issues
GitHub Issues provides similar create functionality

scripts.name: Create Linear Issue
scripts.description: Create a new issue in Linear with title, description, priority, and team

teamKey.description: Team key (e.g., ENG) — resolved to teamId
